page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 24 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory operations and database workloads. The server achieves top 10% placement in large-block read-only and write-only memory bandwidth tests, alongside a top 10% score in PassMark database operations. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark, falls within the average range, while text processing, software compilation, and compression tasks register in the top 25%. Large-model LLM inference tasks present a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing for 70B models scoring in the bottom 10%.

The high density of local NVMe SSD storage combined with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio delivers qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads that require high-speed local caching and substantial memory capacity. This hardware profile is suited for transactional databases, large-scale indexing, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving, though it is less optimal for heavy generative AI text generation workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server powered by 24 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of memory and 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory bandwidth for large blocks and top-tier database operation performance, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. CPU-bound tasks, such as compression and software compilation, perform in the top 25%, while LLM text generation and prompt processing for large models represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 10%. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads, making it suitable for high-throughput databases, indexing, and data warehousing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 24 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized server featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores running at 2.8 GHz
15000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed via the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for transactional databases, data warehousing, and indexing workloads
